title: grastidium pensile
side_effects: Grastidium pensile can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to its spores can also lead to respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma attacks, in individuals with pre-existing conditions. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant can result in skin irritation, including redness and rashes, due to its sap's irritating properties. In severe cases, ingestion of Grastidium pensile can cause liver and kidney damage, requiring immediate medical attention.
